fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the entry, as assigned from the specified number series when the entry was created.

fields.name: Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor account that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies which purchase documents are due today.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of document.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the purchase document number.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the vendor entry.

fields.name: Vendor Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor account that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code for the amount on the line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the entry.

fields.name: Remaining Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that remains to be paid on the purchase documents that are due today.

fields.name: Original Amt. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that the entry originally consisted of, in LCY.

fields.name: Remaining Amt. (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net amount of in the local currency. The amount is calculated using the Remaining Quantity, Line Discount %, and Unit Price (LCY) fields.

fields.name: Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the entry in LCY.

fields.name: Purchase (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the purchase amount on the entry in LCY.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the purchase amount is copied from the Sales/Purch. (LCY) field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the purchase amount is calculated as the sum of the amounts on the lines, minus VAT.

fields.name: Inv. Discount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the invoice discount amount on the entry in LCY.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the amount is copied from the Inv. Discount (LCY) field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the amount is calculated as the sum of all the Inv. Discount Amount fields on the purchase lines.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the buy-from vendor that the entry is linked to.
The number is copied from the Buy-from Vendor No. field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Vendor Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's market type to link business transactions made for the vendor with the appropriate account in the general ledger.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Purchaser Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which purchaser is assigned to the vendor.

fields.name: User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who posted the entry, to be used, for example, in the change log.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the source code that specifies where the entry was created.

fields.name: On Hold
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related entry represents an unpaid invoice for which either a payment suggestion, a reminder, or a finance charge memo exists.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the entry was applied to an already-posted document when it was posted. It does this if the Applies-to Doc. Type and Applies-to Doc. No. fields are filled in before posting. If this is the case, the field shows the type of document that was applied to.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the document type is copied from the Applies-to Doc. Type field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the document type is copied from the Applies-to Doc. Type field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the entry was applied to an already-posted document when it was posted. It does this if the Applies-to Doc. Type and Applies-to Doc. No. fields are filled in before posting. If this is the case, the field shows the number of the document that was applied to.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the number is copied from the Applies-to Doc. No. field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the number is copied from the Applies-to Doc. No. field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Open
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the amount on the entry has been fully paid or there is still a remaining amount that must be applied to.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the purchase document is due.

fields.name: Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the amount in the entry must be paid for a payment discount to be granted.

fields.name: Original Pmt. Disc. Possible
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount that you can obtain if the entry is applied to before the payment discount date.

fields.name: Pmt. Disc. Rcd.(LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the payment discount that has been received on the entry, in LCY.
The program calculates the discount automatically if a payment discount was given during application.

fields.name: Positive
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry to be applied is positive.

fields.name: Closed by Entry No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the entry number of the entry that has been applied to (that has closed) the entry.
The program fills in the field automatically when the entry is applied to.
To see the entries that were applied to by the line, choose Application, Applied Entries.

fields.name: Closed at Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date at which the vendor ledger entry was closed.

fields.name: Closed by Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that the entry was finally applied to (closed) with.

fields.name: Applies-to ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of entries that will be applied to when you choose the Apply Entries action.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the name of the journal that the entry was posted from.
The program fills in the name from the Journal Batch Name on the journal line.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of account that a balancing entry is posted to, such as BANK for a cash account.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the general ledger, customer, vendor, or bank account that the balancing entry is posted to, such as a cash account for cash purchases.

fields.name: Transaction No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the transaction number that is assigned to all the entries in the same transaction. If there are any G/L entries, bank account ledger entries or customer ledger entries associated with the vendor ledger entry, they are assigned the same transaction number.
The number is used to connect all entries that are created by the same posting.

fields.name: Closed by Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount that the entry was finally applied to (closed) with in the local currency.
The program fills in the field automatically when the entry is applied to.
To see the entries that were applied to by the line, click Related Information and then click Applied Entries.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent debits.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent credits.

fields.name: Debit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent debits, expressed in LCY.

fields.name: Credit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total of the ledger entries that represent credits, expressed in LCY.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor entry's document date.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's or vendor's numbering system.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series to which the document number on this entry belongs.

fields.name: Closed by Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code of the entry that was applied to (and closed) this vendor ledger entry.

fields.name: Closed by Currency Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount that was finally applied to (and closed) this vendor ledger entry.

fields.name: Adjusted Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Original Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Original Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the original entry.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval.
Enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Remaining Pmt. Disc. Possible
fields.tooltip: Specifies the remaining payment discount which can be received if the payment is made before the payment discount date.

fields.name: Pmt. Disc. Tolerance Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the latest date the amount in the entry must be paid in order for payment discount tolerance to be granted.

fields.name: Max. Payment Tolerance
fields.tooltip: Specifies the maximum tolerated amount the entry can differ from the amount on the invoice or credit memo.

fields.name: Accepted Payment Tolerance
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the accepted payment tolerance for the entry.

fields.name: Accepted Pmt. Disc. Tolerance
fields.tooltip: Specifies that payment discount tolerance has been accepted for the entry.

fields.name: Pmt. Tolerance (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the accepted payment tolerance for the entry in LCY.

fields.name: Amount to Apply
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount to apply.

fields.name: IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the intercompany partner that the transaction is related to if the entry was created from an intercompany transaction.

fields.name: Applying Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item ledger entry that is applied to the vendor ledger entry.

fields.name: Reversed
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry has been part of a reverse transaction.

fields.name: Reversed by Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the correcting entry that replaced the original entry in the reverse transaction.

fields.name: Reversed Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the original entry that was undone by the reverse transaction.

fields.name: Prepayment
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the vendor ledger entry was posted as a prepayment invoice or credit memo from a purchase order.

fields.name: Creditor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor who sent the purchase invoice.

fields.name: Payment Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment of the purchase invoice.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

fields.name: Applies-to Ext. Doc. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value that you entered in the Applies-to Ext. Doc. No. field in the Payment Journal window.

fields.name: Invoice Received Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the vendor's invoice was received.

fields.name: Recipient Bank Account
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account to transfer the amount to.

fields.name: Message to Recipient
fields.tooltip: Specifies the message exported to the payment file when you use the Export Payments to File function in the Payment Journal window.

fields.name: Exported to Payment File
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the entry was created as a result of exporting a payment journal line.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 3 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 3, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 4 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 4, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 5 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 5, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 6 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 6, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 7 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 7, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 8 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 8, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Remit-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address for the remit-to code.

fields.name: Dispute Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies if there is an ongoing dispute for this document.
